facebook.content_angle: Open water sighting technique — the 'crocodile eyes' drill for pool swimmers transitioning to open water, anchored to the 2026 NZ Open Water Champs at Lake Taupo
facebook.post_text:
Most pool swimmers lose 10+ seconds per km the moment they hit open water. 🌊

It's not fitness. It's sighting — and it's a skill that can be trained.

The **'crocodile eyes' drill** keeps your hips high and stroke rhythm clean. Just lift your eyes barely above the surface. No head lift, no hip drop. 👀

Try it in your next session: take a sighting lift every 8 strokes. Smooth, quick, zero wasted energy.

**Open water shouldn't be where your speed disappears.**

The 2026 NZ Open Water Champs are on the horizon. Start your prep now. 🏆

Tag a training partner who needs this drill 👇
